commands.review_summary:
The branch emits Codex final-answer `answer_candidate` item events, routes them into Control UI Activity with localized labels, and suppresses those candidate events from channel progress.

PR surface: Source +350, Tests +375. Total +725 across 45 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. The PR body and checks provide after-fix Gateway-backed Control UI proof for the projected answer-candidate stream.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is appropriate because no actionable patch defect was found; the remaining action is ordinary maintainer review and merge gating.

summary_lines: The PR adds artifact, installed skill-file, source URL, and verification-envelope fields to ClawHub skill origin/lock metadata while keeping install telemetry restricted to the older version/registry shape.
PR surface: Source +144, Tests +139. Total +283 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction. Source inspection shows current main lacks the richer `.clawhub` provenance fields, and the PR body provides after-patch live output from a ClawHub install.
fixup_lines: PR branch already contained follow-up commit before automerge: Persist ClawHub skill install provenance

target.title: Surface Codex final answer candidates in activity
target.body:
## Summary

- Emit Codex app-server `phase: "final_answer"` answer-candidate activity records with `candidate`, `superseded`, and `selected` states.
- Route those `stream: "item"` / `kind: "answer_candidate"` records into the Control UI Activity model and Activity view, so dashboard users can see the candidate lifecycle instead of losing it.
- Keep answer-candidate activity out of IM/channel progress with `suppressChannelProgress: true`; final channel delivery still uses the selected final reply payload only.
- Localize the new Control UI Activity labels through the existing i18n locale pipeline.

## Real behavior proof

**Current-head live Gateway-backed Control UI proof (June 13, 2026)**

Current PR head: `9c5e6f8acc56` (`fix(codex): gate answer candidate selection on success`).

This update addresses the latest ClawSweeper proof request: show an after-fix Codex app-server event stream traversing the patched Gateway websocket into the actual Control UI Activity surface, not only constructed renderer output.

Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server can stream an early `phase: "final_answer"` candidate, continue with tool work, then produce a later successful final answer. Operator/debug surfaces should show that candidate lifecycle, while messaging channels must still project only one authoritative final reply. This PR wires those candidate events into Control UI Activity and gates `selected` on successful `turn/completed` status.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw repository on macOS, branch `agent/xiaozhua/codex-final-answer-candidates-rebase`, head `9c5e6f8acc56`. The live proof starts the repository test Gateway with `withGatewayServer`, starts the real Control UI e2e server, opens it in Playwright/Chrome, connects the browser to the isolated Gateway websocket, accepts the Control UI Gateway URL confirmation, emits native Codex app-server notifications through the real `CodexAppServerEventProjector`, and lets the Gateway broadcaster deliver `agent` websocket frames into the actual `<openclaw-app>` Activity state and Activity page.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
PLAYWRIGHT_CHROMIUM_EXECUTABLE_PATH="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts \
  ui/src/ui/pr-90610-proof.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Result:

```text
✓ ui-e2e ui/src/ui/pr-90610-proof.e2e.test.ts > PR #90610 real Gateway-backed Control UI proof > renders patched Codex answer_candidate events after traversing Gateway websocket into Control UI Activity 7044ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed (1)
```

Evidence after fix: the proof artifact captured from the browser-side `openclaw-app` after real Gateway websocket delivery contains both the raw Gateway `agent` frames and the resulting Control UI Activity records:

```text
agent event frames received by Control UI eventLogBuffer:
  seq=2 stream=item phase=update status=candidate   progressText="Draft answer" sessionKey=agent:main:main
  seq=3 stream=item phase=end    status=superseded  progressText="Draft answer" sessionKey=agent:main:main
  seq=8 stream=item phase=update status=candidate   progressText="Final answer" sessionKey=agent:main:main
  seq=9 stream=item phase=end    status=selected    progressText="Final answer" sessionKey=agent:main:main

Control UI activityEntries after processing those Gateway events:
  item=msg-early-final    toolName=answer_candidate status=done candidateStatus=superseded summary=answer_candidate.superseded outputPreview="Draft answer"
  item=msg-selected-final toolName=answer_candidate status=done candidateStatus=selected   summary=answer_candidate.selected   outputPreview="Final answer"
```

Visible text captured from the actual Control UI Activity page after expanding the entries included:

```text
Activity
2 of 2
Done answer_candidate
Answer candidate superseded
Item: msg-early-final
Run: run-proof-90610-live-gateway-control-ui
Session: agent:main:main
Draft answer
Done answer_candidate
Answer candidate selected
Item: msg-selected-final
Run: run-proof-90610-live-gateway-control-ui
Session: agent:main:main
Final answer
```

Local artifacts generated by that proof run:

```text
.artifacts/pr-90610/live-gateway-control-ui-proof.json  # browser-captured activityEntries + agentEvents + visibleText
.artifacts/pr-90610/live-gateway-control-ui-proof.png   # screenshot of actual Control UI Activity page, entries expanded
```

Current-head regression proof also passed:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts --testNamePattern="final-answer candidates|non-successful terminal attempts"
# PASS: selected is emitted for successful completed turns; failed/aborted/timed_out/no-turn-completion cases emit [candidate, superseded] and no selected.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts
# PASS: 81 tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern="keeps suppressed answer candidates out of channel item progress"
# PASS: answer-candidate activity stays suppressed from channel item progress.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts --testNamePattern="answer candidates"
# PASS: item events become Control UI activity entries.

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/activity.test.ts --testNamePattern="answer candidate"
# PASS: Control UI Activity renders the answer-candidate entry.

pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm exec oxlint ui/src/ui/activity-model.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts ui/src/ui/views/activity.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/activity.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts
git diff --check
# clean
```

Observed result after fix: a Codex app-server stream with early final-answer candidate → intervening command → later final-answer candidate produces two Control UI Activity records after traversing Gateway websocket delivery. The early candidate is displayed as `Answer candidate superseded`; the later successful final candidate is displayed as `Answer candidate selected`; both show their preview text. Non-successful terminal attempts are covered by regression tests and never emit `selected`. Channel/item-progress delivery remains suppressed for these answer-candidate records, preserving a single final reply payload.

What was not tested: No live Telegram bot message was sent from this PR branch. The live UI proof uses deterministic redacted Codex app-server notifications through the real projector/Gateway/UI path rather than calling the external Codex service.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts --testNamePattern="records superseded and selected final-answer candidates without changing the final reply"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern="keeps suppressed answer candidates out of channel item progress"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts --testNamePattern="records answer candidates in activity from item events"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/activity.test.ts --testNamePattern="renders answer candidate activity with item facts and preview text"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/activity.test.ts`
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `pnpm exec oxlint ui/src/ui/activity-model.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts ui/src/ui/views/activity.ts ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/activity.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/event-projector.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: ooiuuii

commands.author: momothemage

target.title: Persist ClawHub skill install provenance
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #92077.

This PR implements a narrow, local lockfile slice of the ClawHub skill provenance work:

- persists the downloaded ClawHub artifact SHA256/integrity in `.clawhub/lock.json`
- records the installed `SKILL.md` filename and SHA256 after extraction
- snapshots the ClawHub skill verification envelope fields that are useful for audit (`decision`, `reasons`, `card`, `artifact`, `provenance`, `security`, `signature`)
- keeps install telemetry backwards-shaped so the richer local lock data is not sent back to ClawHub telemetry

This intentionally does not close #92077. Source URL requirements, publish-time source validation, pinned install syntax, richer audit commands, and sandbox-by-default remain larger follow-up decisions.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/skills-cli.verify.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: ClawHub skill installs now leave local audit data that ties the install to the downloaded artifact, installed `SKILL.md`, and ClawHub verification response.

Real environment tested: local macOS checkout, Node v22.18.0, pnpm v11.2.2, live `https://clawhub.ai`.

Exact command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'const fs = await import("node:fs/promises"); const os = await import("node:os"); const path = await import("node:path"); const { installSkillFromClawHub } = await import("./src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts"); const root = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), "openclaw-clawhub-lock-proof-")); try { const result = await installSkillFromClawHub({ workspaceDir: root, slug: "openclaw-find-skills", logger: { info: (message) => console.log(`info:${message}`) } }); console.log(`result:${JSON.stringify(result)}`); if (!result.ok) process.exit(1); const lock = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(path.join(root, ".clawhub", "lock.json"), "utf8")); const entry = lock.skills["openclaw-find-skills"]; console.log(`lock:${JSON.stringify({ version: entry.version, artifact: entry.artifact, skillFile: entry.skillFile, verification: entry.verification && { ok: entry.verification.ok, decision: entry.verification.decision, reasons: entry.verification.reasons, provenance: entry.verification.provenance, securityStatus: entry.verification.security?.status } }, null, 2)}`); } finally { await fs.rm(root, { recursive: true, force: true }); }'
```

Observed result after fix:

```json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "artifact": {
    "kind": "archive",
    "sha256": "9fb2b8e43f8301560f50a4484742c51e2d02be1f6a437f4d51e345f970addaca",
    "integrity": "sha256-n7K45D+DAVYPUKRIR0LFHi0Cvh9qQ39NUeNF+XCt2so="
  },
  "skillFile": {
    "path": "SKILL.md",
    "sha256": "6895c10c93ec17ca64b2b06ff15cbfa4278e7a011cb9464d9fc4b90a029af71b"
  },
  "verification": {
    "ok": false,
    "decision": "fail",
    "reasons": ["security.status_not_clean"],
    "provenance": {
      "source": "unavailable",
      "reason": "No server-resolved GitHub import provenance is stored for this version."
    },
    "securityStatus": "suspicious"
  }
}
```

What was not tested: full `openclaw skills audit` / frozen install behavior, because this PR only persists the lock data needed for those later features.
